---
title: "3c Activity: Presentations and Other Outputs"
format:
  html:
    theme: cosmo
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 2
    link-external-newwindow: true
    css: _materials.css
    include-after-body: _progress.html
execute:
  enabled: false
---


## Goal

We will add a Quarto slide deck to the same practice project, reuse generated values and a figure in the slides, render the project, and commit the source files.

Note: The checkboxes on this page are only stored locally in your browser. They are not submitted or synced anywhere.

::: {.guide-progress}

Progress: <span data-progress-count>0</span> / <span data-progress-total>0</span> steps complete.

<button class="reset-progress" type="button" data-progress-reset>Reset progress</button>

:::


## Activity

::: {.panel-tabset}

## 1. Open The Practice Repo

Open the `python_tmr_practice` repository in Codespaces.

This should be the same repository you used for the manuscript and generated-results activities.

::: {.task-list}

<label class="task-check">
  <input type="checkbox" data-progress-id="3c-open-practice-repo">
  <span>I opened the practice repository in Codespaces.</span>
</label>

:::


## 2. Add A Slide Deck

At the top level of the repository, create a file named `slides.qmd`.

Paste this starter deck into the file:

<p class="copy-note">Click inside the block to select it, then copy and paste it into `slides.qmd`.</p>

<textarea class="copy-source" readonly spellcheck="false" rows="52">
&#45;&#45;&#45;
title: "A Short Research Talk"
subtitle: "Python TMR Practice"
author: "Your Name"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: simple
    slide-number: true
    preview-links: false
    link-external-newwindow: true
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
&#45;&#45;&#45;

&#96;&#96;&#96;{python}
#| label: setup-slides

from random import Random

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import pandas as pd
import statsmodels.formula.api as smf
from pandas.plotting import scatter_matrix


rng = Random(2026)

rows = []
for firm_id in range(1, 121):
    size = rng.gauss(0, 1)
    attention = rng.gauss(0, 1)
    slack = 0.35 * size + rng.gauss(0, 0.8)
    performance = (
        0.75 * attention
        + 0.25 * slack
        + 0.20 * size
        + rng.gauss(0, 1)
    )

    rows.append(
        {
            "firm_id": firm_id,
            "attention": attention,
            "slack": slack,
            "size": size,
            "performance": performance,
        }
    )

results_data = pd.DataFrame(rows)
analysis_vars = ["performance", "attention", "slack", "size"]

model = smf.ols(
    "performance ~ attention + slack + size",
    data=results_data,
).fit()

sample_size = len(results_data)
attention_coef = model.params["attention"]
attention_p = model.pvalues["attention"]
attention_coef_text = f"{attention_coef:.2f}"
attention_p_text = "&lt; .001" if attention_p &lt; 0.001 else f"{attention_p:.3f}"
&#96;&#96;&#96;

## Research question

How does stakeholder attention relate to firm performance?


## Data and sample

The sample includes `{python} sample_size` simulated firm-year observations.


## Main result

Attention is positively associated with performance in the simulated data.

b = `{python} attention_coef_text`

p = `{python} attention_p_text`


## Figure

&#96;&#96;&#96;{python}
#| label: fig-slide-scatter-matrix
#| fig-width: 6
#| fig-height: 5
#| out-width: "78%"

axes = scatter_matrix(
    results_data[analysis_vars],
    figsize=(6, 5),
    diagonal="kde",
    alpha=0.7,
)
fig = axes[0, 0].figure
fig.tight_layout()
plt.show()
&#96;&#96;&#96;


## Takeaway

- Quarto can make manuscripts and slides from the same project.
- Generated values help keep research outputs in sync.
- Small workflow changes can reduce copy/paste errors.
</textarea>

Save the file.

::: {.task-list}

<label class="task-check">
  <input type="checkbox" data-progress-id="3c-create-slides-qmd">
  <span>I created `slides.qmd` with the starter deck.</span>
</label>

<label class="task-check">
  <input type="checkbox" data-progress-id="3c-personalize-slides">
  <span>I updated the author name or title in the slide deck.</span>
</label>

:::


## 3. Add Slides To The Project

Open `_quarto.yml`.

In the `render` list, add `slides.qmd`:

```yaml
  render:
    - index.qmd
    - paper.qmd
    - slides.qmd
```

In the navigation list, add a link to the slides:

```yaml
      - text: "Slides"
        href: slides.qmd
```

The full `_quarto.yml` should still include the bibliography, CSL, and HTML format settings from the manuscript activity.

::: {.task-list}

<label class="task-check">
  <input type="checkbox" data-progress-id="3c-add-slides-to-render-list">
  <span>I added `slides.qmd` to the `_quarto.yml` render list.</span>
</label>

<label class="task-check">
  <input type="checkbox" data-progress-id="3c-add-slides-nav">
  <span>I added a Slides link to the website navigation.</span>
</label>

:::


## 4. Link The Slides From The Home Page

Open `index.qmd`.

Add a link to the slide deck under `## Outputs`:

```markdown
- [Research Presentation](slides.qmd)
```

Save the file.

::: {.task-list}

<label class="task-check">
  <input type="checkbox" data-progress-id="3c-link-slides-from-index">
  <span>I linked the slide deck from `index.qmd`.</span>
</label>

:::


## 5. Render And Check

Render the slide deck:

```bash
quarto render slides.qmd
```

Then render the whole project:

```bash
quarto render
```

Open the rendered slide deck and look for:

- the title slide;
- the inline sample size;
- the inline coefficient and p value;
- the scatter matrix figure.

::: {.task-list}

<label class="task-check">
  <input type="checkbox" data-progress-id="3c-render-slides">
  <span>I rendered `slides.qmd`.</span>
</label>

<label class="task-check">
  <input type="checkbox" data-progress-id="3c-render-project">
  <span>I rendered the whole project.</span>
</label>

<label class="task-check">
  <input type="checkbox" data-progress-id="3c-check-slide-output">
  <span>I found the generated values and figure in the rendered slides.</span>
</label>

:::


## 6. Commit And Push

Use Source Control:

1. Review the changes.
1. Stage `slides.qmd`, `_quarto.yml`, and `index.qmd`.
1. Commit with a message such as:

```text
Added Quarto research slides.
```

Push or sync the commit to GitHub.

::: {.task-list}

<label class="task-check">
  <input type="checkbox" data-progress-id="3c-review-slides-diff">
  <span>I reviewed the slide deck and project configuration changes.</span>
</label>

<label class="task-check">
  <input type="checkbox" data-progress-id="3c-commit-slides">
  <span>I committed the slide deck changes.</span>
</label>

<label class="task-check">
  <input type="checkbox" data-progress-id="3c-push-slides">
  <span>I pushed or synced the commit to GitHub.</span>
</label>

:::

:::
